stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Orbán’s defeat in Hungary could unlock €90bn loan for Ukraine, says EU official
stories.summary: Ousted Viktor Orbán had previously blocked releasing funds; Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Iran war means US ‘has no time for Ukraine’. What we know on day 1,512 The change in Hungary’s government could help unlock €90bn for Ukraine and give a “new push” for it to join the European Union, the bloc’s expansion chief said Tuesday. Marta Kos, speaking on the sidelines of the IMF and World Bank spring meetings, described the Hungarian election on Sunday – which saw long-ruling nationalist prime minister V

stories.title: Spain moves to grant legal status to half a million unauthorized immigrants
stories.summary: Spain’s Council of Ministers on Tuesday approved a royal decree that will give legal status to more than 500,000 unauthorized immigrants who currently reside in the country. The move pits Spain’s left-wing coalition government against the center-right People’s Party, which is vowing to challenge the decree in the courts, not to mention the far-right Vox group, which wants the country’s irregular immigrants deported. In an open letter posted on X, Prime Minister Pedro Sánc

stories.title: Ukraine races to secure air defense as global demand surges
stories.summary: BERLIN — Ukraine urgently needs more air defenses but fears surging global demand for interceptors — driven by the Iran war and European rearmament — will leave it short. “There is not enough production capacity in Europe,” Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in Berlin on Thursday. That’s forcing Kyiv to continue relying on the American-made Patriot air defense system, as well as pushing for more French-German SAMP/Ts. Kyiv is even developing its own anti-missile defen

stories.title: Why Orbán’s man in Brussels is here to stay — for now
stories.summary: The EU is eager to move on from Viktor Orbán, but there is one part of his legacy that’s likely to continue: his European commissioner. Health and Animal Welfare Commissioner Olivér Várhelyi is a bona fide Fidesz loyalist who’s been accused of having a spy ring run out of the Hungarian embassy back when he was Budapest’s top envoy to the EU. But he’s also earned a reputation as a hard-working policy wonk in a town where technocrats run the show — and where the EU executive operates on the

stories.title: Zelenskyy hails Magyar’s win over Orbán as ‘the victory of light over darkness’ in Hungary – as it happened
stories.summary: Ukrainian leader hopes for ‘pragmatic’ and ‘friendly’ relations with new government in contrast with hostility of previous pro-Russian regime in Berlin At his press conference with Zelenskyy, Germany’s chancellor Friedrich Merz also welcomed Péter Magyar’s decisive victory, saying it would have “implications for our support for Ukraine”. “More Hungarians than ever before cast their votes. By an overwhelming majority, they voted not only to oust a government, but to oust an entire system.” “Volod

stories.title: In a joyful Budapest, I see the chance of an unprecedented transition | Timothy Garton Ash
stories.summary: With the ejection of Trumpian hero Viktor Orbán, Hungarians demanded a restored democracy. Now, Europe must support them To be in Budapest last Sunday evening was to see history again being made on the Danube. As rapturous crowds gathered on the riverbank opposite the brightly illuminated parliament building, chanting “Ria-ria Hungaria!” and “Hungary-Europe!”, we all knew that the implications of the dramatic election victory for the Tisza party of Péter Magyar go far beyond this one central Eur

stories.title: US military says it killed four more people in a boat strike in the eastern Pacific
stories.summary: Strike marks third deadly attack on vessels in region in four days, and the killing of 174 people since September Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The US military said it killed four more people in a boat strike in the eastern Pacific ocean on Tuesday, marking the third deadly attack on vessels in the region in four days. The US Southern Command, which oversees military operations in Latin America and the Caribbean, announced the killings in a social

stories.title: Is the EU back in vogue? – podcast
stories.summary: Lisa O’Carroll reports on the ‘resetting’ of the relationship between the UK and the EU This week, the Guardian reported that Labour is planning to bring in new legislation that will forge closer ties between the UK and the EU . Nearly 10 years on from the Brexit vote, the Guardian’s senior correspondent Lisa O’Carroll speaks to Helen Pidd about what a UK-EU reset would look like. Lisa and Helen also discuss the strength of the EU in the wake of Viktor Orbán’s defeat in the Hungary elections on

stories.title: Viktor Orbán inspired rightwingers across the EU and in Britain. His defeat could represent a turning of the tide | Polly Toynbee
stories.summary: We must hope this vote will be the start of a wider backlash – and send hard-right populism back to the fringes where it belongs The forces of darkness rolled back on Sunday. The mighty combined power of Vladimir Putin’s Russia and Donald Trump’s America were defeated in Hungary, as European liberal democratic values triumphed . The populist-nativist right put their all into keeping Viktor Orbán in power. The US vice-president, JD Vance, mid-war in Iran, took time out to parade his patronage in

stories.title: Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales resign from Congress amid sexual misconduct allegations
stories.summary: Departures came after lawmakers from both parties threatened to introduce resolutions expelling the two men US politics live – latest updates Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Democratic congressman Eric Swalwell and Republican congressman Tony Gonzales submitted their resignations to the House of Representatives on Tuesday, abruptly ending their political careers amid bipartisan furor over allegations of sexual misconduct against both. Swalwell resigned at 2pm ET, while Gona

stories.title: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
stories.summary: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

stories.title: Orban's defeat in Hungary reverberates across Central Europe
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

stories.title: After Orbán loss, Europe’s far right reassesses MAGA endorsement
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s defeat in the Hungarian elections suggests that political endorsements from Donald Trump or JD Vance do not necessarily boost European far-right campaigns, as the MAGA message increasingly struggles to resonate in Europe.

stories.title: Zsiday Viktor szerint azért erősödik a forint, mert a támogatás és a politikai akarat is megvan az euró bevezetésére
stories.summary: A közgazdász szerint az a várakozás áll a jelenség hátterében, hogy Magyarország belátható időn belül belép az euróövezetbe, és onnantól fogva nincs forint.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor belesétált saját propagandája csapdájába, és maga alá temette rendszerét
stories.summary: Ezúttal nem az ellenzék, hanem a Fidesz buboréka volt az, amelyik egyáltalán nem érintkezett már a valósággal. Orbán Viktor tényleg elhitte, hogy győzni fog. Nem a bukás mértéke, hanem a módja miatt tűnik lehetetlennek, hogy ebből is felálljon. Eloszlatta saját tévedhetetlensége mítoszát.

stories.title: Spiró Györgyöt negyven évvel ezelőtt „ocsmányság” érte – egy kritika
stories.summary: A frissen kiadott 1986-os naplójában szó esik a születendő gyerekéről is, de arról még inkább, hogy egy tanulmányban lengyelellenesnek titulálták. Kritika.

stories.title: Haladásról számoltak be az Európai Bizottság elleni magyar kémkedés gyanújának vizsgálatában
stories.summary: Részleteket nem árultak el, így nem derült ki, hogy az eredmény kapcsolódik-e a testület magyar biztosának pozíciójához.

stories.title: Reagált a Külügy az iratmegsemmisítések vádjára: Nonszensz, Szijjártó Péter és Gyarmati Eszter nem is járt azokban az irodákban
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a nemzetközi sajtótájékoztatóján jelentette be, hogy információik szerint Szijjártó Péter szankciós anyagokhoz köthető dokumentumokat semmisíti meg a kollégáival a Külügyminisztériumban.

stories.title: Nagy Márton bankstopja ellenére lengyel bank jöhet Magyarországra
stories.summary: A PKO Bank Polski vezetése felkapta a fejét a magyarországi választások hírére.

stories.title: Óriási roham kell az uniós pénzekért az új kormánytól
stories.summary: Néhány hónap maradt, hogy pénzt lehessen menteni a helyreállítási alapból. Magyar Péter kedden azt írta, Von der Leyentől ígéretet kapott a bizottsági együttműködésre, „hogy a rendkívül szoros határidőn belül sikerüljön eredményeket elérni és a magyar emberek mielőbb hozzájuthassanak az őket megillető uniós fejlesztési forrásokhoz”.

stories.title: A másnap után: Pamacs kutya füle és a vén gorilla között már bevillant Magyar Péter is a TV2 Tényekben
stories.summary: A hétfői öt perces belpolitikai blokk nyolc percesre hízott, de ideje menni a nyári gumit cseréltetni, és a súlyadót is be kellene fizetni.

stories.title: „A barátom volt” – Trump először reagált Orbán Viktor vereségére
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök csak második nekifutásra reagált magyar szövetségese választási vereségére, hétfőn még hallgatott.

stories.title: Az Európai Központi Bank vezetője üdvözölte, hogy Magyar Péter bevezetné az eurót
stories.summary: Christine Lagarde szerint az euró bevezetése időigényes folyamat, amihez számos gazdasági és jogi reform szükséges.

stories.title: Hajdú Péter elmagyarázta, mire gondolt, mikor függetlennek nevezte magát
stories.summary: A műsorvezető a Fidesz eredményvárójáról nyilatkozva állította magáról, hogy független.

stories.title: Elkezdték visszapótolni a mélypontra süllyedt olaj- és üzemanyag-készleteket
stories.summary: Történelmi mélypontra süllyedt a magyar olaj és üzemanyag biztonsági készletének mennyisége.

stories.title: Kinevették a Századvég vezető elemzőjét egy konferencián, amin a hatalmasat tévedő méréseiket magyarázta
stories.summary: A Századvég politikai elemzési igazgatója szerint Magyar Péter azért nyerhette meg a választást, mert az embereket leginkább foglalkoztató problémákkal kampányolt.

stories.title: A Tisza-kormányon is múlik, bóvliba süllyed-e Magyarország
stories.summary: Az S&P Global Ratings hitelminősítő írta le, mi kell a lecsúszás elkerüléséhez.

stories.title: Megnéztük a közmédia kedd esti híradóját, és nem hittük el, amit láttunk
stories.summary: Magyar Péter mint megválasztott kormányfő, hiteles források, és még Deák Dániel is bocsánatot kért. A közszolgálati média híradója két nappal a választások után újra közszolgálati híradóként kezdett viselkedni.

stories.title: Azt ismerik, hogy a lengyel robot elkezdi kergetni a vaddisznókat?
stories.summary: Nem? Pedig ez a videó tarolja le épp a világhálót!

stories.title: A választás előtt két héttel megszüntették a Magyar Péterrel összefüggésbe hozott, bennfentes kereskedelemről szóló büntetőeljárást
stories.summary: A döntésről valamiért csak később, a választások után számoltak be.

stories.title: What next for Hungary as country celebrates Péter Magyar victory over Viktor Orbán?
stories.summary: People in the capital, Budapest, and across Hungary celebrated the victory of the opposition party Tisza, led by Péter Magyar, which has won the parliamentary elections, putting an end to the 16-year rule of the populist Viktor Orbán. The Guardian's Europe reporter Jakub Krupa reports from the Hungarian capital Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: Poland and South Korea upgrade ties to strategic defence partnership deal
stories.summary: During a visit to Seoul on Monday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung pledged to strengthen their cooperation, particularly in defence.

stories.title: 'We cannot ask any country to give up its territory,' Magyar says on Ukraine
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Viktor Orbán had largely centred his electoral campaign on Ukraine and Volodymyr Zelenskyy, blaming Kyiv for the Druzhba pipeline blockade to accusations of meddling in Hungarian elections. Will things change with Magyar in power?

stories.title: Vance’s bad week: vice-president risks becoming face of two Trump foreign policy failures
stories.summary: Orbán is out in Hungary and talks have failed to end the war in Iran – ill-fated road trip has been setback for Maga aims Shortly before JD Vance’s ill-fated week crisscrossing the world, Donald Trump asked him during a private Easter brunch about how the Iran negotiations were shaping up. “If it doesn’t happen, I’m blaming JD Vance,” Trump said to laughs in the room. “If it does happen, I’m taking full credit.” The joke at Vance’s expense contained an unfortunate nugget of truth: this is not an

stories.title: A román miniszterelnök tanácsadója arra kéri Magyar Pétert, vizsgálják ki a Romániába juttatott közpénzek sorsát is
stories.summary: A korábbi EP-képviselő levelet írt Magyar Péternek.

stories.title: Egy új, pozitív fejezetben bíznak a nemzetközi befektetők, akik elkezdték felmérni az Orbán utáni országot
stories.summary: Sokat várnak az EU-s kapcsolatok javulásától, illetve az ennek következményeként felszabaduló források hatásától.

stories.title: Franciaország egyik legrangosabb múzeumában nyílt önálló kiállítása Ferenczy Károlynak
stories.summary: A magyar modernizmus kiemelkedő alkotója először mutatkozik be nagyszabású, monografikus kiállítás keretében külföldön.

stories.title: Az utolsó héten még neki kampányolt, mégse lepődött meg J. D. Vance Orbán vereségén
stories.summary: Az amerikai alelnök szerint nem azért látogatott Budapestre, mert biztosak voltak Orbán győzelmében.

stories.title: A Roszatom vezetője Magyarról: Ismeri a paksi projektet, hiszen Orbán kormányában dolgozott
stories.summary: Alekszej Lihacsov fenntartja mérsékelt optimizmusát a paksi bővítés folytatásával kapcsolatban, egy olyan életrajzi elemet említve a Tisza Párt vezetőjéről, amely valójában nem igaz, hiszen Magyar Péternek nem volt kormányzati pozíciója.

stories.title: Von der Leyen és Magyar Péter újra tárgyaltak, az EB elnöke szerint gyorsan kell dolgozni a jogállamiság helyreállításán
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen ismét egyeztetett Magyar Péterrel.

stories.title: Mészáros Lőrinc rengeteg szerződést kötött, csodálatos lehetőség, hogy most megmutassa, hogyan sikerült ez neki – interjú Ferencz Orsolya űrbiztossal
stories.summary: Kiket kellene kivágni a nemzeti oldal testéből a Fidesz veresége után? A választás másnapján nagyot futó videót készített, de konkrétumokkal nem, legfeljebb kilátásba helyezésekkel lettünk gazdagabbak az interjú során.

stories.title: Hadházy a Telexnek: Nagyon könnyű lenne lecsukni Orbánt, Lázárt és Szijjártót, csak szándék kérdése
stories.summary: A leköszönő képviselő a Telex élő műsorában arról is beszélt, hogyan élte meg a Tisza győzelmét, és folytatja-e a korrupcióellenes munkáját.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a közmédiában: reggel fél nyolckor a Kossuth rádióban kezd, aztán az M1 vendége lesz
stories.summary: „A szemünk előtt hullik darabjaira a pártállam” – írta a meghívásról posztjában a Tisza Párt elnöke, akit másfél éve láthattunk utoljára a közmédiában.

stories.title: Advisor to Romanian PM asks Péter Magyar to investigate how Hungarian public funds sent to Romania were spent
stories.summary: Vlad Gheorghe said that there are indications that Hungarian public funds were used to finance pro-Orbán political propaganda outside Hungary, including in Romanian communities.

stories.title: A környező országok elkerülték azt a benzincsapdát, amibe mi belesétáltunk
stories.summary: Csak a Magyarországon bevezetett védett ár nem hagyott teret arra, hogy az üzemanyagimport nyereséges maradjon.

stories.title: Az IMF jelentősen rontotta a magyar gazdaság idei kilátásaira vonatkozó előrejelzését
stories.summary: És rontott az idei globális növekedési előrejelzésén is a valutaalap: a januári prognózishoz képest kisebb ütemben nőhet a globális gazdaság.

stories.title: Zelenszkij szerint április végéig újra tudják indítani a Barátság kőolajvezetéket
stories.summary: A kormány korábban azzal is vádolta Zelenszkijt, hogy a vezeték elzárásával a vasárnapi magyar választást akarta befolyásolni.

stories.title: Magyar békefenntartó katonák indultak Libanonba
stories.summary: Mivel a közel-keleti biztonsági helyzet jelentősen romlott, a katonák emelt szintű felkészítésen vettek részt előtte.

stories.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
stories.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban's election defeat sparks hope and relief
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major changes.

stories.title: EU applauds Hungary’s election result
stories.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

stories.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
stories.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.title: Hungarian voters celebrate 'much-needed' change
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by 45-year-old ex-party insider Péter Magyar.

stories.title: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.title: Thousands celebrate in Hungary as Péter Magyar ends Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule – video
stories.summary: Thousands flooded the streets as Hungary's opposition leader, Péter Magyar, declared victory after his Tisza party claimed a landslide win over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party. The victory ends Orbán's 16-year rule and marks a significant shift for Hungary's relationship with the EU and Russia. Orbán was celebrated by conservatives across Europe and the US, but lost favour at home with voters who grew weary of economic stagnation, international isolation and oligarchs amassing wealth. His landslide

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
stories.summary: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

stories.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
stories.summary: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: Sheinbaum vows to ‘defend Mexicans at every level’ amid anger at Trump over migrant deaths
stories.summary: Sheinbaum has recently been taking a firmer stance with the US, defying pressures where other countries have caved The Mexican government has voiced concern about the deaths of its citizens in US custody, with Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum also pushing back against the Trump administration’s decision to impose an energy blockade on Cuba. The progressive Mexican leader has walked a careful line with Trump for more than a year, addressing provocations with a measured tone and meeting US requ

stories.title: Labor’s controversial algorithm tool for aged care under investigation by ombudsman
stories.summary: Exclusive: It follows numerous complaints made to Guardian Australia, politicians and advocacy organisations about the Integrated Assessment Tool Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The commonwealth ombudsman is investigating the government’s algorithm-based aged care assessment tool, which has been described by assessors as “cruel” and “inhumane” in its determination of home support funding for elderly Australians.

stories.title: NAACP lawsuit accuses Elon Musk’s xAI of polluting Black neighborhoods near Memphis
stories.summary: Suit alleges the billionaire’s AI company is illegally spewing toxic pollutants from its datacenter in the Memphis area A new lawsuit accuses Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company of illegally spewing toxic pollutants into the Black neighborhoods on the border of Tennessee and Mississippi. The suit, filed on Tuesday in Mississippi federal court, alleges xAI is violating the Clean Air Act due to emissions from its makeshift power plant in Southaven, Mississippi, which powers its datacenters

stories.title: US-Iran peace talks could resume in next two days, Trump says
stories.summary: US president says negotiations could restart in Islamabad under ‘fantastic’ Pakistani army chief Asim Munir • Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has said that US-Iranian peace talks could resume in Islamabad over the next two days, and complimented the work of Pakistan’s army chief as mediator. The US president was speaking on Tuesday to a New York Post reporter who had gone to Islamabad for the first round of ceasefire talks over the weekend. After an interview discussing prospects

stories.title: Germany's aid to Ukraine faces challenges
stories.summary: At Ukrainian-German government talks, Berlin reassures Kyiv it can continue counting on military, civilian, and humanitarian aid, even as political opposition and US cuts create challenges for sustained support.

stories.title: Former von der Leyen aide’s new role reflects EU competition policy shift
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Ursula von der Leyen wants to modernize competition policy so that European businesses can stand their ground against global rivals, and with the appointment of Anthony Whelan she has installed a trusted adviser to make that happen. Whelan, an Irish national who advised the Commission president on digital policy for her first six years in office, was named on Monday to lead the EU executive’s competition department. The move puts a seasoned eurocrat with deep policy knowledge and prov

stories.title: Irish government loses key rural voice in surprise resignation amid fuel-price fury
stories.summary: DUBLIN — Ireland’s government suffered a surprise blow Tuesday over its wobbly handling of weeklong fuel-price protests by farmers and truckers — who, over a pint, persuaded a government minister to resign. The surprise announcement from the junior agriculture minister, Michael Healy-Rae , came during a debate of no confidence in the government of Prime Minister Micheál Martin, whose year-old center-right administration depends on rural independent lawmakers like Healy-Rae to maintain its majori

stories.title: French police barred from raiding Macron’s palace to investigate Panthéon contracts
stories.summary: PARIS — The French presidency refused to let investigators enter the Elysée Palace on Tuesday as part of a probe into contracts linked to memorial ceremonies, invoking the immunity enjoyed by French President Emmanuel Macron. The investigators that presented themselves at the Elysée Palace weren’t granted entry, according to a French presidency official who was granted anonymity for protocol reasons. “Investigators … were told that the documents pertaining to Élysée Palace staf

stories.title: ‘Debased himself’ and risking Australia’s reputation: Liberals torn up about Taylor’s Trumpian immigration plan
stories.summary: Howard-era former minister Amanda Vanstone criticises parts of hardline policy but backs English language requirement Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former Howard government minister Amanda Vanstone has warned Angus Taylor against turning immigration into heavy-handed law enforcement , saying most migrants from countries run by dictators and extremists move to Australia to escape authoritarianism. Releasing the

stories.title: Trump accuses ally Meloni of lacking courage for not joining attacks on Iran
stories.summary: Remarks come as Italian PM suspends defence agreement with Israel amid growing domestic pressure over conflict Donald Trump lashed out at one of his closest allies on Tuesday, saying Italy’s Giorgia Meloni lacked courage in light of her failure to join the US in attacking Iran. “I’m shocked at her. I thought she had courage, but I was wrong,” the US president said in an interview with the Italian newspaper Corriere della Sera. Continue reading...

stories.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea

The decision by EU lawmakers and member states after late night talks on Monday, will reduce duty-free quotas by 47%. Exact country allocations have yet to be determined. Continue reading...

Ebben a Kelet-Európai konyhákat egy nagy rokonságként képzeli el, amit összeköt a káposzta.

stories.title: A PSG Liverpoolban is győzött, a Barcelona győzelemmel esett ki a BL-ben
stories.summary: A Bajnokok Ligája kedd esti negyeddöntőiből a címvédő PSG és az Atlético Madrid jutott tovább. Előbbi a Liverpoolt búcsúztatta, utóbbi a Barcelonát, ami most sem úszta meg kiállítás nélkül.

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood says Southport inquiry report exposed ‘systematic failures across multiple public sector organisations’ – as it happened
stories.summary: The home secretary made the comments after an inquiry revealed that the system ‘completely failed’ Keir Starmer has confirmed that he wants to stop children being exposed to addictive scrolling features on their phones as part of measures to protect them from social media. The PM is under pressure to implement an Australian-style ban on social media for under-16s, and the government is consulting on whether to go ahead with a full ban, or whether to just impose more specific restrictions. It’s n

stories.title: Chagos Islands treaty is now ‘impossible to agree at political level’, UK minister says
stories.summary: Stephen Doughty says US withdrawal of support means bill cannot complete passage through parliament A treaty over ceding sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius has become “impossible to agree at political level” and the corresponding bill will not complete its passage through parliament, a Foreign Office minister has said. Stephen Doughty told the Commons that the agreement with Mauritius was initially negotiated in close coordination with the US, but Donald Trump’s position “appears to

stories.title: US starts naval blockade of Iranian ports after deadline passes
stories.summary: Iran warns Americans they face higher pump prices due to prohibition imposed on Monday evening Middle East crisis – live updates The US blockade of ships using Iranian ports in the Gulf has come into effect, turning the six-week-old conflict between the US-Israeli coalition and Iran into a test of economic endurance. US Central Command (Centcom) made no formal announcement of the start of the blockade but had said it begin on Monday at 5.30pm Iranian time and would apply to any ships entering or

stories.title: Top US Senate Democrats again try for war powers resolution – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed – our live coverage continues here Circling back to Donald Trump’s coming naval blockade, the US military said it would block all Iranian Gulf ports on Monday at 10am ET on Monday (5.30pm in Iran and 1400 GMT) , effectively seizing control of maritime traffic in the strait of Hormuz. “The blockade will be enforced impartially against vessels of all nations entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas, including all Iranian ports on the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Om

stories.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
stories.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

stories.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
stories.summary: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

stories.title: Trump's naval blockade raises economic pressure on Iran
stories.summary: A US naval blockade cuts off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will President Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

stories.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV in ongoing feud with Catholic Church leader over Iran war
stories.summary: The US president launches an unprecedented attack on Pope Leo XIV over the pontiff's rejection of the Iran war, accusing him of being "weak on crime" and hurting the Catholic Church.

stories.title: Germany cuts fuel tax as oil prices surge on Iran blockade
stories.summary: Following tense exchanges between Economics Minister Reiche and Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil, the government held talks over the weekend. Will the coalition succeed in easing the burden on citizens, or will it collapse?

stories.title: Union Berlin pushes back on sexist comments aimed at trailblazing coach Marie-Louise Eta
stories.summary: Eta has been named interim coach for the last five games of the season as Union aims to avoid relegation from Germany's Bundesliga.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.summary: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV says he has 'no fear' of Trump administration
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV said he has no intention to debate with Donald Trump after the US president criticised him over his comments on the Iran war. "I have no fear of the Trump administration nor of speaking out loudly about the message of the gospel," the pope told reporters aboard the papal plane.

stories.title: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.title: Up to 200 civilians killed in Nigeria after air force 'misfire' on market, sources say
stories.summary: Such misfires are common in Nigeria, where the military often conducts air raids to battle armed groups who control vast forest enclaves.

stories.title: Judge dimisses Trump's lawsuit against WSJ over reporting on ties to Epstein
stories.summary: The judge, who was appointed by former president Barack Obama, gave Trump the opportunity to amend his complaint and refile the suit by 27 April.

stories.title: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands
stories.summary: Rapidly strengthening storm brings destructive winds, flooding risk and dangerous seas to western Pacific Dangerous super typhoon barreling toward group of remote US islands The Mariana Islands archipelago in the western Pacific, home to the US territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, are bracing for extreme weather early this week as Super Typhoon Sinlaku approaches. The system originated as a cluster of thunderstorms over the seas of Micronesia before strengthening into a tropical

stories.title: What is the new EU bill and could it give UK ministers Henry VIII-type powers?
stories.summary: Legislation would allow government to implement evolving single market rules without full parliamentary scrutiny Ministers in Britain are planning a new bill that would bring into force a food and drink trade deal with the EU but also contain powers enabling the government to “dynamically align” with Europe. It would allow the UK to quickly implement evolving single market rules if it determines it is in the national interest, without having to face full parliamentary scrutiny. Continue reading.

stories.title: Pope Leo visits Algeria in sign of Africa’s growing importance to Catholic church
stories.summary: Pontiff makes first papal visit to country as he starts 11-day tour that will also include stops in Cameroon and Angola Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Algeria for the first papal visit to the country, calling for peace on the opening stop of a tour of Africa that signals the continent’s growing importance to the Catholic church. The 11-day trip, which will include stops in Cameroon, Angola and Equatorial Guinea, is the longest by Pope Leo since being elected to the papacy in May last year. Continue

stories.title: Renewed ties with EU needed to boost UK security and economy, says Starmer
stories.summary: PM says lessons must be learned from shocks to cost of living as government plans to align with bloc’s rules by default The economic and security benefits of a closer relationship with the EU are “simply too big to ignore”, Keir Starmer has told parliament as the British government prepares for more rapid alignment with European rules. Updating MPs on the Iran conflict and his visit to the Gulf last week, the prime minister was explicit about what he argued was the need for renewed ties with Eur

stories.title: Bahamas police again question US man over disappearance of wife at sea
stories.summary: Brian Hooker says wife Lynette fell overboard from dinghy but family members have cast doubt on that account Police in the Bahamas on Monday were set to again interview a US man who said his wife fell overboard from their boat. In a statement on Sunday to the Guardian, Brian Hooker’s attorney, Terrel Butler, said: “The police have requested another interview with [Brian Hooker] tomorrow.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Esterházy Péter születésnapján két friss könyvvel idézhetjük fel, hogy neki a művészet volt a világnézete
stories.summary: A friss interjúkötetben az író fociról, gasztronómiáról, gyereknevelésről és közügyekről beszél, az esszéiben pedig a nyelv csodáiról.

stories.title: Azbeszttel szennyezett kőzúzalékkal borították le Szombathely egyik városrészét, büntetőeljárás indult
stories.summary: A kőzetanyagot osztrák bányákból szállították a városba, az Oladi Plató nevű városrész összes utcája érintett lehet.

stories.title: Katy Perry sajtósa szerint totális kamu, hogy az énekesnő húsz éve szexuálisan zaklatott egy ausztrál színésznőt
stories.summary: Ruby Rose nemrég arról írt, egy melbourne-i szórakozóhelyen Perry az arcához dörgölte a nemi szervét, amikor még csak 20 éves volt.

stories.title: Hamar új korszakra ébredt a Petőfi TV a választások után
stories.summary: A Petőfi TV műsorvezetője április 13-án azt mondta, szeretnének nyitni azok felé az alkotók felé is, akik eddig még nem szerepeltek náluk.

stories.title: Republikon: A Tisza az országjárással a kisebb településeken is átvette a vezetést
stories.summary: Csak az 500 lakos alatti kistelepüléseken maradt meg a Fidesz markáns előnye.

stories.title: Néhány millió éve óriás hangyászsünök flangáltak Ausztráliában
stories.summary: Az állatok akkorák voltak, mint egy gyerek.

stories.title: Kilép a DK-ból Kiss László óbudai polgármester
stories.summary: Nyilvános posztokban támogatta a kampányban a Tisza Pártot, úgyhogy kizárása előtt inkább elhagyja a DK-t.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz says fuel tax lowered to ease price surge
stories.summary: Berlin is cutting fuel tax and arms manufacturer Rheinmetall is preparing to build missiles in a new venture. Elsewhere, a fisherman triggered a massive search before being found drunk and asleep in a car. DW has more.

stories.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
stories.summary: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guinness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

stories.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
stories.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

stories.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
stories.summary: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

stories.title: Haiti: at least 30 dead in stampede at UNESCO‑listed Citadelle Laferrière fortress
stories.summary: At least 30 people died on Saturday, 11 April 2026, after a stampede at the Citadelle Laferrière near Milot, a major tourist site in the north of Haiti.

stories.title: “I’m not a fan of Pope Leo”: Trump criticises pontiff over Iran comments
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump criticised Pope Leo XIV following comments on the Iran conflict, calling him “very liberal” and saying he was “not a fan” of the pontiff.

stories.title: US to blockade Iranian ports, risking wider war over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US‑Iran talks in Pakistan have collapsed as Trump announced the blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz starting on Monday afternoon, stoking war fears and pushing oil prices sharply higher.
